hero.desc: It affects your skin, weight, mood, and fertility. Let's treat the whole you.
hero.longDesc: If your cycles are unpredictable, your acne will not calm down, or your body feels like it is working against you, you are not being dramatic. At Nexus Clinic Kuala Lumpur, we build a realistic PCOS plan that fits your goals, your schedule, and your body.

trust.t2Desc: you feel daily, including weight and insulin resistance, acne, unwanted hair, and cycle tracking
trust.t3Desc: guided by the 2023 International PCOS Guideline (Rotterdam criteria and treatment pathways)
trust.t4Desc: LG 10, Lower Ground Floor, Wisma UOA II, Jalan Pinang, 50450 Kuala Lumpur

whatIs.title: What Is PCOS
whatIs.desc1: PCOS (Polycystic Ovary Syndrome) is a common hormone condition in women of reproductive age. It is linked to irregular ovulation, higher androgens (male-type hormones), and sometimes ovaries that look "polycystic" on ultrasound.
whatIs.desc2: Important detail: PCOS does not always mean you have true cysts. Many sources explain the ovaries may contain many small follicles, and "cysts" is often a confusing word for patients.
whatIs.stat: 13% globally using Rotterdam criteria, and it may be higher in some regions including South East Asia.
whatIs.imageCaption: Understanding PCOS is the first step to healing

symptoms.title: Signs and Symptoms
symptoms.subtitle: That Make Women Search "PCOS Treatment Kuala Lumpur"
symptoms.desc: PCOS can look different from one woman to another. Common symptoms include:
symptoms.s1: Irregular periods (late, missed, or very long cycles)
symptoms.s4: Weight gain or difficulty losing weight
symptoms.mentalNote: PCOS can also affect mental health. Some Malaysian hospital education pages mention depression, anxiety, and even disordered eating patterns in women with PCOS.
symptoms.empathy: If you read that and thought "That is me," you deserve care that takes it seriously.

lifestyle.title: Step 1: Lifestyle That Actually Helps
lifestyle.subtitle: (Without Shame)
lifestyle.desc: This is not about "just lose weight." It is about improving insulin sensitivity and reducing hormone chaos.
lifestyle.evidence: Even small changes can help. NHS guidance notes that in overweight women, losing around 5% of body weight can lead to significant improvement in PCOS. Malaysian hospital pages also highlight regular exercise, a balanced diet, and weight management as core strategies.
lifestyle.h2: Build muscle 2 to 3 times a week (home workouts count)
lifestyle.h3: Prioritise protein and fibre in meals
lifestyle.h4: Reduce sugary drinks and frequent desserts (not forever, just less often)
lifestyle.h5: Sleep earlier when possible (PCOS and poor sleep feed each other)
lifestyle.note: This is the base that makes medication work better, and makes symptoms return less often.
lifestyle.step2Desc: Combined oral contraceptive pills and other hormone options

hair.title: Step 4: Unwanted Hair and Hair Thinning
hair.subtitle: (Hirsutism and PCOS)
hair.desc: Unwanted facial hair is one of the most emotionally heavy PCOS symptoms. The guideline even notes the psychosocial impact and encourages clinicians to take patient reporting seriously.
hair.opt2: Anti-androgens in selected patients, with strong contraception guidance
hair.opt3: Laser and light hair reduction (more sessions may be needed in PCOS)

insulin.title: Step 5: Insulin Resistance and Weight Support
insulin.desc: Many women with PCOS feel like they eat less and still gain weight. Insulin resistance can be part of that story.
insulin.metforminDesc: Can be used in certain PCOS situations, including anovulatory infertility. May be considered over inositol for hirsutism and central adiposity.
insulin.glp1Desc: GLP-1 medications in selected PCOS patients under medical care, depending on BMI and health history.

fertility.title: Step 6: PCOS and Fertility
fertility.subtitle: (If You Want to Get Pregnant)
fertility.desc: PCOS is one of the most common causes of infertility, but it is also treatable. Many women with PCOS can still get pregnant.
fertility.ovulationDesc: Letrozole should be first-line pharmacological treatment for ovulation induction in infertile anovulatory women with PCOS.
fertility.plan1: Cycle tracking and timed intercourse guidance
fertility.plan3: Referral to fertility specialists if needed

cost.title: Cost of PCOS Treatment
cost.subtitle: (Realistic, Not Guessy)
cost.desc: PCOS costs vary because PCOS is not one treatment.
cost.disclaimer: Your exact cost depends on what you actually need, not what a package includes.

phases.title: What PCOS Treatment Looks Like
phases.desc: Think of your care in phases:
phases.phase1Desc: You bring symptoms. We confirm what's going on using guideline-based criteria and sensible testing.
phases.phase2: Phase 2: Build your plan around your main goal
phases.phase2Desc: Think of your care in phases:
phases.phase3Desc: PCOS changes over time. Your plan should too.

competitor.title: Competitor Snapshot
competitor.c1Item1: Pantai Hospitals: clear symptoms list, diagnosis approach
competitor.c1Item2: Gleneagles: OCPs for cycle regulation and hirsutism
competitor.c1Item4: KL women specialist: weight-loss with metformin and GLP-1
competitor.c1Item5: Fertility centres: PCOS and fertility FAQs

faq.a1: PCOS usually cannot be 'cured,' but symptoms can be treated and controlled long term.
faq.q2: What is the best treatment for PCOS?
faq.a2: There is no single best treatment. The best plan depends on your goal: cycle control, acne and hair, weight and insulin resistance, or fertility. The international guideline recommends tailored care based on symptoms and life stage.
faq.q3: Can I get pregnant if I have PCOS?
faq.a3: Yes. Many women with PCOS can get pregnant, sometimes naturally and sometimes with ovulation support.
faq.q4: What is the first-line fertility medication for PCOS?
faq.a4: The 2023 International PCOS Guideline summary states letrozole should be first-line for ovulation induction in infertile anovulatory women with PCOS (with no other infertility factors).
faq.q5: How is PCOS diagnosed in adults?
faq.a5: Diagnosis uses revised Rotterdam criteria, usually requiring two of: hyperandrogenism, ovulatory dysfunction, or polycystic ovaries on ultrasound, while excluding other conditions like thyroid disease and high prolactin.
faq.q6: Do I need an ultrasound to confirm PCOS?
faq.a6: Not always. The guideline notes diagnosis can often be made based on clinical features, and ultrasound or AMH may be used in adults depending on the diagnostic pathway.
faq.q7: Why do I gain weight so easily with PCOS?
faq.a7: Insulin resistance is a common driver in PCOS. It can increase hunger, cravings, and fat storage, and can worsen hormone imbalance.
faq.q8: How much weight loss helps PCOS symptoms?
faq.a8: Even modest changes can help. NHS guidance notes that around 5% weight loss can significantly improve PCOS symptoms in overweight women.
faq.a9: Metformin may help in selected PCOS patients, especially where insulin resistance or anovulatory infertility is involved, and the guideline highlights counselling about GI side effects.
faq.q10: Is inositol better than metformin for PCOS?
faq.a10: The guideline notes metformin should be considered over inositol for hirsutism and central adiposity, and encourages women using complementary therapies to tell their health professional.
faq.q11: What is the best treatment for unwanted facial hair in PCOS?
faq.a11: Options include hormonal approaches and hair-reduction methods. The guideline specifically states laser and light therapies should be considered for facial hirsutism and quality of life, and more sessions may be needed in PCOS.
faq.a12: Yes, acne is a common symptom in PCOS. Malaysian hospital pages list acne as a PCOS symptom, and the guideline recommends assessing acne as part of hyperandrogenism review.
faq.q13: Should I worry about long-term health if I have PCOS?
faq.a13: PCOS is linked with metabolic risks and mental health concerns, so long-term monitoring and early lifestyle support matter.
faq.q14: Where can I get PCOS support in Kuala Lumpur?
faq.a14: Many women start at hospitals and O&G clinics for diagnosis. If your main concerns include weight, insulin resistance, acne, and unwanted hair, Nexus Clinic Kuala Lumpur is centrally located at Wisma UOA II, Jalan Pinang.
